mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with the 3-month annualized growth rate of RSAFS over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid and significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and impending economic weakness, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, thus negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period
evidence_refs: fact:historical recessions often preceded by rising unemployment
failure_condition: If, after the UNRATE trigger, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: An environment of very low federal funds rates combined with strong housing starts indicates an accommodative monetary policy effectively stimulating demand-side growth, which boosts investor confidence and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and HOUST 3-month average growth is above 5% year-over-year
failure_condition: If, when both conditions are met, the average 20-day forward return is negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.1) over any 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic expansion, strong industrial production growth signifies increasing output and capacity utilization, leading to higher corporate earnings and driving S&P 500 forward returns higher over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month average growth is above 0.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production correlates with economic cycles
failure_condition: If the positive relationship between INDPRO growth and SPX 60-day returns disappears or reverses (correlation < 0) during periods officially classified as economic expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, provides the Federal Reserve with flexibility to maintain accommodative monetary policy, which supports higher equity valuations by lowering discount rates and encouraging investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:low inflation allows for lower interest rates
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns show a statistically significant negative correlation with the 12-month percentage change in CPIAUCSL over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in real GDP (GDPC1) signals a weakening economy or recession, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and a subsequent decline in S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth is negative for two consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:recessions are often defined by GDP contraction
failure_condition: If, following two consecutive quarters of negative GDPC1 growth, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of sustained positive employment growth (PAYEMS) and increasing retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a strong and healthy consumer-driven economy, which directly translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth is above 0.1% month-over-month and RSAFS 3-month average growth is above 0.2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:employment and sales are key economic health indicators
failure_condition: If, when both employment and retail sales show positive growth, the average 20-day forward return is negative or not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.1) over any 4-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high and rising inflation, the Federal Reserve is compelled to tighten monetary policy, leading to higher interest rates that increase the cost of capital and reduce equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month percentage change is above 3% and FEDFUNDS has increased by more than 0.25% over the last 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Fed raises rates to combat inflation
regime_hint: high inflation and monetary tightening environment
failure_condition: If, during periods where CPIAUCSL is above 3% YoY and FEDFUNDS is rising as per the trigger, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.5%) over a 5-year period.
